name: Nut Rocha
recipeIngredient: 1 package Milk chocolate pieces (11 1/2 oz, about 1 1/4 cups)
1 cup Finely chopped toasted nuts (such as almonds, pecans, walnuts, and/or cashews)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 15 x 10 x 1 inch baking pan with foil, extending over the edges.
2. In a 3-quart saucepan, melt butter. Stir in sugar, corn syrup, and 1/3 cup water. Cook over medium-high heat to boiling, stirring until the sugar is dissolved. Avoid splashing onto the sides of the pan.
3. Attach a candy thermometer to the pan, ensuring the bulb is well covered and not touching the bottom of the pan. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the thermometer registers 290°F (soft-crack stage), about 15 minutes. The mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over the entire surface.
4. Remove the thermometer and pour the mixture into the prepared pan, spreading it evenly. Allow to cool for 5 minutes or until the top is just set.
5. Sprinkle chocolate pieces on top; let stand for 2 minutes. Spread the chocolate onto the toffee. Top with chopped nuts and press them into the chocolate.
6. Allow to cool several hours until set. If necessary, place in the refrigerator.
7. Carefully lift the foil from the pan using tongs or a spatula. Break the candy into pieces. Store in an airtight container between sheets of wax paper. Makes about 2 1/2 lbs.
